summary: Renamed fighter motion flag constants across the fighter subsystem from the long FtStateChange_* prefix to the shorter Ft_MF_* convention. The PR touched 67 files, including ft/forward.h, common and character forward headers, motion state tables, and special-move source files. The change appears to be a naming-only cleanup performed with melee-replace-symbols, preserving bit values and existing MotionFlags compositions while making the constants align with the existing ftCo_MF_* and character-specific *_MF_* naming style.
what_changed: Replaced the global fighter motion flag prefix FtStateChange_* with Ft_MF_* in src/melee/ft/forward.h.

Preserved all MotionFlags bit assignments in ft/forward.h, e.g. FtStateChange_None became Ft_MF_None at value 0, FtStateChange_PreserveFastFall became Ft_MF_KeepFastFall at 1 << 0, and FtStateChange_FreezeState became Ft_MF_FreezeState at 1 << 21.
Renamed Preserve* flag names to Keep* names, including KeepFastFall, KeepGfx, KeepSfx, KeepAccessory, KeepSwordTrail, KeepColAnimHitStatus, and KeepColAnimPartHitStatus.

Renamed SkipUpdate* flag names to shorter Skip* names, including SkipHit, SkipModel, SkipAnimVel, SkipMatAnim, SkipParasol, SkipRumble, SkipColAnim, SkipItemVis, SkipModelPartVis, SkipModelFlags, SkipHitStun, and SkipAnim.
Renamed unknown-number flags to compact names such as Ft_MF_Unk06, Ft_MF_Unk19, Ft_MF_Unk24, Ft_MF_Unk27, Ft_MF_Unk30, and Ft_MF_Unk31.
Updated common composite MotionFlags in ftCommon/forward.h, such as ftCo_MF_Squat, ftCo_MF_Attack, ftCo_MF_Special, ftCo_MF_GuardReflect, item-swing flags, and landing/throw/down-state flag groups.
Updated character-specific MotionFlags constants in many chara forward.h files, including CLink zair flags and Captain special-move flags.
Updated motion state table flag fields in initializer files such as ftCl_Init.c, ftCh_Init.c, ftMh_Init.c, and multiple fighter init files.
Updated local transition_flags constants in special-move source files such as ftCa_SpecialN.c and ftCa_SpecialS.c to use the new Ft_MF_* names.
Used an explicit symbol replacement script in the PR body invoking cargo run -rp melee-replace-symbols with one old:new mapping per motion flag.
smart_moves: Kept the change mechanical and scope-limited: the diff is a broad rename but does not show behavioral edits to flag values or logic.
Centralized the convention shift in src/melee/ft/forward.h first, then propagated to composite flag constants and call sites.
Used an auditable replacement map in the PR body, making it easy to verify every old FtStateChange_* name against its new Ft_MF_* name.
Chose names that reflect the existing MotionFlags abbreviation pattern already visible in composite constants like ftCo_MF_* and ftCa_MF_*.
Shortened noisy names in large bitmask expressions, improving readability in motion state tables and special-move transition flag compositions.
Improved semantics by replacing the ambiguous FtStateChange prefix with Ft_MF, making clear these are motion flags rather than state-change functions or enum states.
decomp_lessons: For widely used constants, a mechanical rename with an explicit old-to-new mapping is safer and easier to review than hand-editing scattered call sites.
Use subsystem-consistent abbreviations: MotionFlags constants now use the MF abbreviation already used by ftCo_MF_* and character-specific motion flag composites.
Bitflag names should describe the effect at the flag level, not only the context where they are passed. Ft_MF_SkipModel is clearer in expressions than FtStateChange_SkipUpdateModel.
Keep unknown flags clearly marked as unknown while preserving bit identity; this PR retained Unk names but normalized formatting to Unk06, Unk19, etc.
Large header cleanup can reduce future decomp friction even without changing matching, because shorter and more regular names make bitmask compositions easier to compare.
When renaming decomp symbols, preserve comments and bit positions in the defining header so future semantic discoveries can update names without losing provenance.
naming_conventions: Global fighter motion flag constants use Ft_MF_*.
Composite common fighter motion flag constants continue to use ftCo_MF_*.
Character-specific composite motion flag constants continue to use per-character prefixes such as ftCa_MF_* and ftCl_MF_*.
Use Keep* for flags that preserve existing state, replacing Preserve*.
Use Skip* for flags that skip an update, replacing SkipUpdate*.
Use compact zero-padded names for one-digit unknown flag numbers, e.g. Ft_MF_Unk06 instead of FtStateChange_Unk_6.
Use plural when the target is plural in meaning: Ft_MF_SkipModelFlags replaced FtStateChange_SkipUpdateModelFlag.
Keep semantically uncertain flags marked with Unk, e.g. Ft_MF_UnkUpdatePhys.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-specific matching tactic is evident from the provided PR context.
The practical matching tactic was a symbol-only mechanical rename using cargo run -rp melee-replace-symbols, which should preserve generated code when identifiers do not affect layout or constants.

Because all changed constants remain static MotionFlags const values with the same bit shifts, this kind of cleanup should be safe for matching if no macro expansion or type/layout changes are introduced.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR dump.
The PR was merged quickly on the same day it was opened, but no reviewer rationale is available in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that assign semantics to Ft_MF_Unk06, Ft_MF_Unk19, Ft_MF_Unk24, Ft_MF_Unk27, Ft_MF_Unk30, or Ft_MF_Unk31.
Search for subsequent renames of Ft_MF_UnkUpdatePhys or comments around its behavior.
Check whether later fighter cleanup PRs further normalize ftCo_MF_* composite flag names.
Look for PRs using melee-replace-symbols for broad decomp naming migrations.
Search for changes to MotionFlags type definitions or motion state table layouts after PR 815.
